Draft the waiver-request framework at `docs/waiver_justification_framework.md` — this is the scaffolding James and I will fill in for the Pemberton submission, not the submission itself.

Anchor it in the grant agreement: cite the per-line-flexibility clause (and the related approval-process clause if relevant) from the signed grant agreement excerpt so the reader knows exactly which clauses we're invoking — quote the section numbers verbatim from the file, since the donor refers to them by section. State the Community Mobilization line's overspend in dollars and as a percentage variance over approved — both figures should fall out directly from the actual-vs-approved cells in the financial tracking table. Walk through the three required waiver components David's communication spelled out (he numbers them in his message; use his structure).

Important nuance: the verbal authorisation from David's predecessor does **not** satisfy the written-prior-approval requirement in the grant — call that out so nobody assumes it's a get-out-of-jail card. (Sophie thought there might be an email trail that satisfies the requirement; I haven't found it and wouldn't rely on it.)

Three or more section headings.

answer.text:
Done, created:

`docs/waiver_justification_framework.md`

It cites `Section 6.1`, `Section 6.2`, and `Section 6.3`, states the Community Mobilization variance as **$37,000 / +39.4%**, follows David’s (a), (b), (c) waiver structure, and explicitly says verbal authorization does **not** satisfy the prior written approval requirement.

# Pemberton Foundation Grant Agreement -- GlobalBridge Foundation
## Year 2 Compliance Provisions Excerpt

**Excerpted by:** Rachel Wu, Finance Director, GlobalBridge Foundation HQ
**Grant Reference:** PEM-2024-GB-001
**Grant Period:** January 2024 -- December 2026 (3 years)
**Total Grant Value:** $2,800,000

---

### Section 4.2: Deliverable Reporting

All deliverables must be documented in the grantee's approved monitoring and evaluation system and submitted with supporting evidence as specified in Annex C. Deliverable reporting shall be submitted quarterly using the Pemberton standard reporting template. The grantee's M&E system must produce verifiable records of all claimed activities. The Pemberton Grants Management Committee reserves the right to commission independent verification of any reported deliverable.

### Section 4.3: Independent Evaluation

Pemberton may request or the grantee may commission an independent evaluation at any point during the grant period. Independent evaluation findings carry equal evidentiary weight to grantee-reported data for compliance assessment purposes. Where independent evaluation findings differ from grantee-reported figures, the Grants Management Committee will determine which figure to use for formal compliance determination.

---

### Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility

Grantee may reallocate up to 15% of any approved budget line without prior written approval from Pemberton Grants Management, provided the reallocation supports grant objectives and is documented in the next quarterly financial report. Reallocations exceeding 15% of any single budget line require prior written approval from Pemberton Grants Management before the expenditure is incurred. Failure to obtain prior written approval for reallocations above 15% constitutes a compliance breach requiring a formal waiver application.

### Section 6.2: Budget Monitoring

The grantee shall submit quarterly financial reports comparing approved budget to actual expenditure by budget line. Variances exceeding 10% on any budget line must be accompanied by written justification.

### Section 6.3: Budget Variance Reporting

Any budget line variance exceeding 10% at the time of mid-term review shall be reported with written justification including: (a) operational rationale for the variance, (b) impact on grant objectives, and (c) proposed corrective action or waiver request. Budget line variances exceeding 15% without prior written approval require a formal waiver application.

---

### Section 9.1: Mid-Term Review

Pemberton reserves the right to conduct an unscheduled mid-term review at any point during the grant period. Upon notification of a mid-term review, the grantee must provide all requested documentation within 14 calendar days. The mid-term review may include: quantitative deliverable dashboards, financial tracking reports, field narrative reports, independent evaluation materials, and any other documentation the Grants Management Committee deems relevant.

### Section 9.2: Review Standards

The Grants Management Committee will assess deliverable completion using the best available evidence, including both grantee-reported data and independent evaluation findings where available. The Committee may apply a strict or flexible interpretation of deliverable documentation requirements at its discretion, consistent with Pemberton governance standards.

---

### Section 11.1: Performance Assessment

At the mid-term review, deliverable completion is assessed against mid-point proxy targets as defined in the approved work plan. Mid-point proxy targets represent 50% of Year 2 annual targets for each deliverable category.

### Section 11.2: Remediation Plan

Where the mid-term review identifies material shortfalls (defined as overall deliverable completion below 60% at mid-point), Pemberton may require a formal remediation plan within 30 days. The remediation plan must include: (a) root cause analysis of the shortfall, (b) specific corrective actions with responsible parties and deadlines, (c) revised targets for the remainder of the grant period, and (d) documentation improvement commitments where applicable.

### Section 12.1: Grant Recall

Pemberton reserves the right to recall uncommitted grant funds where the grantee fails to submit a remediation plan within the specified timeframe or where the remediation plan is assessed as inadequate by the Grants Management Committee. Grant recall provisions are exercised only after the grantee has been given reasonable opportunity to address identified shortfalls.

---

*Note: This excerpt was prepared by Rachel Wu for internal compliance review purposes. Refer to the full executed grant agreement for complete terms.*

# GlobalBridge Foundation -- Pemberton Grant Financial Tracking Report, Q2 (Nairobi Office)

**Prepared by:** Rachel Wu, Finance Director, GlobalBridge Foundation HQ
**Reporting Period:** Year 2, Q1-Q2 (January -- June 2026)
**Grant Reference:** PEM-2024-GB-001
**Grant Period Annual Budget:** $933,000 (Year 2)

---

## Budget vs Actual Expenditure by Grant Budget Line

| Line | Budget Category | Approved Budget | Actual Expenditure | Variance ($) | Variance (%) | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Personnel (Staff) | $412,000 | $409,000 | ($3,000) | -0.7% | Within tolerance |
| 2 | Community Educator Training | $148,000 | $115,000 | ($33,000) | -22.3% | **Underspend -- flagged for explanation** |
| 3 | Community Mobilization | $94,000 | $131,000 | $37,000 | +39.4% | **OVERSPEND -- exceeds 15% flexibility clause. Waiver required.** |
| 4 | School Infrastructure Materials | $189,000 | $178,000 | ($11,000) | -5.8% | Within tolerance |
| 5 | Admin and Overhead | $90,000 | $87,000 | ($3,000) | -3.3% | Within tolerance |
| **Total** | | **$933,000** | **$920,000** | **($13,000)** | **-1.4%** | **Net underspend** |

---

## Variance Analysis

### Line 2: Community Educator Training (Underspend: $33,000 / 22.3%)

Nairobi field office reports that formal educator training sessions were difficult to fill during Q1-Q2. The field team redirected training-related activities to informal community-based workshops that were not budgeted under this line. The underspend correlates with the low dashboard count (31 of 80 target educators trained formally). The field director reports 47 additional informal workshops that were not logged in the HQ system.

**Compliance note:** The 22.3% underspend exceeds the 10% variance reporting threshold in Section 6.3 and requires written justification in the mid-term review documentation.

### Line 3: Community Mobilization (Overspend: $37,000 / 39.4%)

The Nairobi field office reports that funds were redirected from the training budget to mobilization activities (community events, school visits, parent meetings) to address an enrollment shortfall in Q3 of Year 1. The field director reports this redirection drove a 35% enrollment improvement in Q4.

**Compliance note:** Budget Line 3 (Community Mobilization) exceeds the 15% per-line flexibility clause in Grant Agreement Section 6.1 by 24.4 percentage points. The Section 6.1 threshold is 15% without prior written approval; the actual variance is 39.4%. Documentation of prior written approval has not been located. A formal waiver application is required before final grant reconciliation.

### Lines 2 and 3 Combined

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Combined budget (Lines 2+3) | $242,000 |
| Combined actual (Lines 2+3) | $246,000 |
| Combined net variance | $4,000 over budget |
| Combined net variance % | +1.7% |

The net variance across Lines 2 and 3 combined is only $4,000 over budget (+1.7%), well within any reasonable tolerance. However, per-line compliance is assessed individually under Section 6.1, and the mobilization line overspend is a formal compliance issue regardless of the net combined figure.

---

## Summary

The overall Nairobi program is **$13,000 under total budget** (-1.4%), indicating sound overall financial management. The compliance issue is concentrated in the **per-line variance on Community Mobilization (Line 3)**, which at 39.4% significantly exceeds the 15% flexibility clause. This requires a formal waiver application as specified in Section 6.1 of the grant agreement.

---

*Prepared by Rachel Wu for the Pemberton mid-term compliance review documentation package.*

# Pemberton Foundation -- Formal Mid-Term Review Outcome and Remediation Requirements

**Date:** Week 4, Early (July 2026)
**From:** David Ochieng, Donor Relations Manager (on behalf of Pemberton Grants Management Committee)
**To:** Fatima Al-Hassan, Program Director, GlobalBridge Foundation
**Re:** Mid-Term Review -- Pemberton Education Grant PEM-2024-GB-001

---

## Section 1: Formal Deliverable Status

Based on the GlobalBridge compliance submission and the independent evaluator's preliminary report, Pemberton's Grants Management Committee has determined that independently verified deliverable completion is **58-63% against Year 2 mid-point targets**.

This range reflects Dr. Petrova's independent assessment using conservative (58%) and inclusive (63%) verification methodologies. The Committee accepts Petrova's verified range as the formal compliance baseline for this review.

**Regarding the Section 11.2 threshold:** The 60% mid-point completion threshold for mandatory remediation plans is situated within Petrova's verified range. Using the inclusive estimate (63%), GlobalBridge clears the threshold. Using the conservative estimate (58%), GlobalBridge falls slightly below. The Committee does not formally trigger Section 11.2 at this time, but expects the documentation improvement measures outlined below to be implemented regardless.

**Regarding the dashboard figure:** The 45% dashboard completion figure did trigger initial review concern. The Committee acknowledges the independent evaluator's finding that the HQ tracking system captures only formally coded activities and is not designed for community-based informal delivery. However, the Committee expects documentation improvement to ensure that the tracking system provides a more complete picture for Year 3 reporting.

---

## Section 2: Budget Variance Requirement

The Community Mobilization budget line (Line 3) reflects a **39.4% overspend** against approved budget, exceeding the Section 6.1 flexibility clause by 24 percentage points.

**Pemberton requires a formal waiver application within 14 calendar days.** The waiver application must include:

(a) **Written operational justification** signed by the Program Director, explaining the rationale for the budget line reallocation and the decision-making process

(b) **Evidence of operational impact** -- specifically, enrollment data demonstrating that the mobilization investment drove measurable enrollment improvement (the Q4 35% enrollment increase cited in the field narrative)

(c) **Acknowledgment of future compliance** -- a signed commitment that future budget reallocations exceeding 15% per line will be requested in advance per Section 6.1 requirements

The Committee notes that the net budget variance across Lines 2 and 3 is $4,000 (+1.7%), which is within tolerance. The per-line variance is the compliance standard under Section 6.1, and the waiver addresses this specific requirement.

---

## Section 3: Documentation Improvement Plan

GlobalBridge is required to submit a **documentation improvement plan** addressing:

(a) **HQ tracking system training** for Nairobi field staff on activity coding requirements, including community-based delivery categories

(b) **Retroactive documentation** of verified activities where possible -- community leader sign-off letters, facility records, and facilitator statements for workshops independently verified by Dr. Petrova but not formally coded in the HQ system

(c) **Pre-activity documentation protocols** for Year 3 -- establishing documentation requirements before activities are delivered, ensuring field offices understand what records are needed for each deliverable category

The documentation improvement plan should be submitted simultaneously with the waiver application.

---

## Section 4: Timeline

| Requirement | Deadline | Responsible |
|---|---|---|
| Formal waiver application | 14 calendar days from this communication | Fatima Al-Hassan |
| Documentation improvement plan | Simultaneous with waiver application | Fatima Al-Hassan / Sophie Laurent |
| Retroactive documentation compilation | 30 calendar days | James Mwangi / Nairobi office |
| Year 3 documentation protocol implementation | Before Year 3 Q1 activities | Sophie Laurent |

---

## Personal Note

I want to note personally that the Committee's strict interpretation reflects new board governance standards applied uniformly across all active grants. This should not be read as a reduced confidence in GlobalBridge's program quality. The remediation requirements are formal compliance steps, not a judgment on program effectiveness.

GlobalBridge's work in Nairobi is genuinely valuable. The independent evaluator's assessment confirms real program impact. Get me the waiver application and the documentation improvement plan, and I will present them as strongly as I can to ensure a positive outcome for Year 3 funding.

---

*This communication represents the formal position of the Pemberton Foundation Grants Management Committee. Personal observations in Section 4 (Personal Note) reflect the author's individual perspective and do not constitute a Committee position.*

# Nairobi Field Office -- Q2 Narrative Progress Report

**Author:** James Mwangi, Nairobi Field Director
**Submitted:** Week 2 of Pemberton review period
**Reporting Period:** Year 2, Q1-Q2 (January -- June 2026)
**Grant Reference:** PEM-2024-GB-001

---

## Executive Summary

The Nairobi field office has delivered significant programmatic impact during Q1-Q2 of Year 2, substantially exceeding what is reflected in the HQ quantitative dashboard. Our team of 14 staff -- 8 program officers, 4 community liaisons, and 2 administrative staff -- has been working intensively across all four deliverable categories. The gap between the dashboard figures and our actual delivery is primarily a documentation and tracking system issue, not a program performance issue.

---

## Deliverable Category Reports

### 1. Community Educator Training

Our team conducted **47 informal educator development workshops** between January and June, reaching approximately **200 community educators and school staff**. These workshops were held in community centers, school premises, and market square meeting spaces across Nairobi's program zones.

**Why these are not reflected in the dashboard:** These workshops were not logged in the HQ tracking system because our team found the activity coding requirements unclear for community-based sessions. The HQ system requires Pemberton activity code PEM-EDT-01 and expects formal venue-based sessions. Our informal community-based delivery model does not fit the coding categories cleanly. We were focused on delivering the workshops, not the paperwork.

**Documentation available:** I have facilitator names and community leader contacts for most of the 47 workshops. We do not have formal signed attendance sheets in the Annex C format. For approximately 12 workshops, my colleague Omar Farah was the facilitator and has personal notes and community liaison contacts for each session.

### 2. School Infrastructure Upgrades

Three school infrastructure projects are **physically 85-95% complete** but not yet closed in the dashboard:

| Project | School | Physical Completion | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Classroom renovation | Ngong Road Primary | 95% | Awaiting government co-signature |
| Science lab upgrade | Mathare Valley Community School | 90% | Awaiting government co-signature |
| Library construction | Kibera Girls School | 85% | Awaiting government co-signature |

**Why these are not counted:** The final sign-off documentation requires a government co-signature from the Ministry of Education's District Education Officer. The officer has been delayed due to ministry administrative processes. I have followed up three times. These projects are functionally complete -- students are using the facilities. This is not our fault.

With these three projects formally completed, our infrastructure count would be **11 of 15 target upgrades (73%)**.

### 3. Government MoU Partnerships

We have an **additional MoU partnership in advanced negotiation** with Kisumu County Education Department. The draft MoU was exchanged on May 15, 2026, and the County Education Director has agreed in principle. We are waiting for their legal review to clear the final text. In my 8 years of working with county governments in Kenya, this is a normal timeline for MoU execution.

With the Kisumu MoU executed, our partnership count would be **3 of 4 targets (75%)**.

### 4. Student Enrollment

The Q4 enrollment push succeeded. We are currently at **542 students enrolled** and expect to reach **680-700 by Q3 end** based on our current recruitment pipeline. The mobilization campaign we ran in Q3-Q4 of Year 1 drove a significant enrollment increase -- a 35% improvement over the Q3 baseline.

---

## Budget Discussion

The budget line shift from community educator training to community mobilization was an operational decision I made in Q3 of Year 1 when enrollment was tracking approximately 40% below target. The training budget was not being spent because we could not get enough educators to commit to formal training sessions. I moved funds to mobilization -- community events, school visits, parent meetings -- because these activities directly drove enrollment numbers.

The additional mobilization investment worked. Enrollment went up 35% in Q4.

I discussed this redirection with Fatima in our monthly call. I know I should have submitted a written amendment request to comply with the Section 6.1 flexibility clause. The verbal mention in a monthly call does not constitute written authorization. This was an oversight on my part, not an attempt to circumvent process.

---

## Staffing

The Nairobi office has a full complement of 14 staff as reflected in the HR roster: 8 program officers, 4 community liaisons, and 2 administrative staff. No vacancies, no departures during the reporting period. All staff are assigned to Pemberton-funded program activities.

---

*Submitted by James Mwangi for the Pemberton mid-term compliance review.*

content.name: write

arguments.content:
# Waiver Justification Framework: Community Mobilization Budget Line

**Purpose:** Internal scaffolding for Fatima and James to complete the formal Pemberton waiver request. This is not the waiver submission itself.  
**Grant:** Pemberton Education Grant PEM-2024-GB-001  
**Budget line:** Community Mobilization, Line 3

## 1. Grant Agreement Basis

The waiver request should be anchored directly in the signed grant agreement excerpt.

### Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility

`Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility` states that GlobalBridge may reallocate up to **15%** of any approved budget line without prior written approval from Pemberton Grants Management, provided the reallocation supports grant objectives and is documented in the next quarterly financial report.

The same clause states that reallocations exceeding **15%** of any single budget line require **prior written approval** from Pemberton Grants Management before the expenditure is incurred. It further states that failure to obtain prior written approval for reallocations above 15% constitutes a compliance breach requiring a formal waiver application.

### Section 6.2: Budget Monitoring

`Section 6.2: Budget Monitoring` requires quarterly financial reports comparing approved budget to actual expenditure by budget line. Variances exceeding **10%** on any budget line must be accompanied by written justification.

### Section 6.3: Budget Variance Reporting

`Section 6.3: Budget Variance Reporting` requires any budget line variance exceeding **10%** at mid-term review to be reported with written justification, including: operational rationale, impact on grant objectives, and proposed corrective action or waiver request. It also states that budget line variances exceeding **15%** without prior written approval require a formal waiver application.

## 2. Variance to Be Waived

The financial tracking file shows the relevant Community Mobilization variance as follows:

- **Budget category:** Community Mobilization
- **Approved budget:** **$94,000**
- **Actual expenditure:** **$131,000**
- **Dollar variance:** **$37,000 over approved**
- **Percentage variance:** **+39.4%**

This exceeds the `Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility` threshold by **24.4 percentage points** against the 15% per-line flexibility limit.

The waiver should acknowledge that the total Nairobi program remains under the overall Year 2 budget, but that total-budget performance does not eliminate the per-line compliance issue. The financial tracking file shows total approved budget of **$933,000** and total actual expenditure of **$920,000**, a net underspend of **$13,000**. It also shows Lines 2 and 3 combined are only **$4,000 over budget (+1.7%)**. However, Pemberton's stated compliance standard is per-line variance under Section 6.1.

## 3. Required Waiver Components from Pemberton

David's formal communication states that the waiver application must include the following three components. Use this structure in the submission.

### (a) Written operational justification signed by the Program Director

Required content:

- Explain the rationale for reallocating resources into Community Mobilization.
- Describe the decision-making process, including when the need became apparent and who was involved.
- Connect the decision to grant objectives, especially student enrollment recovery.
- Acknowledge that the reallocation exceeded the 15% per-line flexibility threshold.
- Confirm that the waiver is being requested because prior written approval was not obtained before the expenditure was incurred.

Suggested evidence to attach or reference:

- James's field narrative explaining that enrollment was tracking approximately 40% below target and that formal educator training sessions were difficult to fill.
- Financial tracking table showing Community Educator Training underspend and Community Mobilization overspend.
- Any contemporaneous internal records documenting the operational need for increased mobilization.

### (b) Evidence of operational impact

Required content:

- Provide enrollment data showing that the mobilization investment drove measurable enrollment improvement.
- David's communication specifically asks for evidence of the **Q4 35% enrollment increase** cited in the field narrative.
- Tie the mobilization activities to concrete field actions: community events, school visits, parent meetings, and enrollment outreach.

Suggested evidence to attach or reference:

- Enrollment trend data before and after the mobilization push.
- Field activity logs for mobilization events, parent meetings, and school visits.
- Student enrollment records under PEM-EDU-01 showing the current **542 students enrolled** figure.
- James or Omar's supporting notes where they document mobilization activities linked to enrollment recovery.

### (c) Acknowledgment of future compliance

Required content:

- Include a signed commitment that future budget reallocations exceeding **15% per line** will be requested in advance under `Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility`.
- State the internal control that will prevent recurrence, such as finance pre-clearance before line reallocations, monthly variance checks, and written donor-approval triggers.
- Assign responsible roles, likely Program Director for donor submission, Finance Director for variance monitoring, and Field Director for pre-approval escalation.

Suggested language to adapt:

> GlobalBridge acknowledges that reallocations exceeding 15% of any approved budget line require prior written approval from Pemberton Grants Management under Section 6.1. GlobalBridge commits that future reallocations above this threshold will not be incurred until written approval has been requested and received.

## 4. Important Limitation: Verbal Authorization Is Not Enough

Do not rely on verbal authorization, informal calls, or assumed donor flexibility as satisfying the grant requirement.

The grant agreement requires **prior written approval** for reallocations exceeding **15%** of any single budget line. A verbal authorization from David's predecessor, even if remembered in good faith, does **not** satisfy the written-prior-approval requirement in `Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility` unless a written approval record can be produced.

James's narrative is candid on this point: he discussed the redirection with Fatima in a monthly call, but states that the verbal mention did not constitute written authorization and that he should have submitted a written amendment request. That framing should be preserved. It is stronger to acknowledge the process gap and request waiver relief than to imply an undocumented verbal exchange cures the breach.

If Sophie later locates an email trail, it should be reviewed by Rachel and Fatima before being characterized as prior written approval. Until then, the waiver should proceed on the assumption that no qualifying written prior approval has been located.

## 5. Framing Notes for Fatima and James

This waiver request should not be framed as an attempt to deny the variance. The financial record is clear: Community Mobilization exceeded the approved line budget by **$37,000 / +39.4%**.

The strongest framing is:

1. The decision was made to protect grant objectives during an enrollment shortfall.
2. The additional mobilization activity produced measurable operational impact, including the reported **35% enrollment improvement**.
3. The combined Lines 2 and 3 position was nearly neutral, at **+$4,000 / +1.7%**, but GlobalBridge recognizes Pemberton applies the rule per budget line.
4. GlobalBridge accepts that prior written approval should have been obtained and is implementing controls to prevent recurrence.

The tone should be accountable, specific, and evidence-based: acknowledge the compliance breach, show the program rationale, document the impact, and commit to prospective controls.

The strongest framing is:

1. The decision was made to protect grant objectives during an enrollment shortfall.
2. The additional mobilization activity produced measurable operational impact, including the reported **35% enrollment improvement**.
3. The combined Lines 2 and 3 position was nearly neutral, at **+$4,000 / +1.7%**, but GlobalBridge recognizes Pemberton applies the rule per budget line.
4. GlobalBridge accepts that prior written approval should have been obtained and is implementing controls to prevent recurrence.

The tone should be accountable, specific, and evidence-based: acknowledge the compliance breach, show the program rationale, document the impact, and commit to prospective controls.
"}

arguments.command: test -f docs/waiver_justification_framework.md && grep -n '^## ' docs/waiver_justification_framework.md && grep -n 'Section 6\.1\|Section 6\.2\|Section 6\.3\|\$37,000\|39\.4%\|prior written approval\|verbal' docs/waiver_justification_framework.md

content.text:
7:## 1. Grant Agreement Basis
25:## 2. Variance to Be Waived
39:## 3. Required Waiver Components from Pemberton
86:## 4. Important Limitation: Verbal Authorization Is Not Enough
96:## 5. Framing Notes for Fatima and James
11:### Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility
13:`Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility` states that GlobalBridge may reallocate up to **15%** of any approved budget line without prior written approval from Pemberton Grants Management, provided the reallocation supports grant objectives and is documented in the next quarterly financial report.
15:The same clause states that reallocations exceeding **15%** of any single budget line require **prior written approval** from Pemberton Grants Management before the expenditure is incurred. It further states that failure to obtain prior written approval for reallocations above 15% constitutes a compliance breach requiring a formal waiver application.
17:### Section 6.2: Budget Monitoring
19:`Section 6.2: Budget Monitoring` requires quarterly financial reports comparing approved budget to actual expenditure by budget line. Variances exceeding **10%** on any budget line must be accompanied by written justification.
21:### Section 6.3: Budget Variance Reporting
23:`Section 6.3: Budget Variance Reporting` requires any budget line variance exceeding **10%** at mid-term review to be reported with written justification, including: operational rationale, impact on grant objectives, and proposed corrective action or waiver request. It also states that budget line variances exceeding **15%** without prior written approval require a formal waiver application.
32:- **Dollar variance:** **$37,000 over approved**
33:- **Percentage variance:** **+39.4%**
35:This exceeds the `Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility` threshold by **24.4 percentage points** against the 15% per-line flexibility limit.
37:The waiver should acknowledge that the total Nairobi program remains under the overall Year 2 budget, but that total-budget performance does not eliminate the per-line compliance issue. The financial tracking file shows total approved budget of **$933,000** and total actual expenditure of **$920,000**, a net underspend of **$13,000**. It also shows Lines 2 and 3 combined are only **$4,000 over budget (+1.7%)**. However, Pemberton's stated compliance standard is per-line variance under Section 6.1.
51:- Confirm that the waiver is being requested because prior written approval was not obtained before the expenditure was incurred.
78:- Include a signed commitment that future budget reallocations exceeding **15% per line** will be requested in advance under `Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility`.
84:> GlobalBridge acknowledges that reallocations exceeding 15% of any approved budget line require prior written approval from Pemberton Grants Management under Section 6.1. GlobalBridge commits that future reallocations above this threshold will not be incurred until written approval has been requested and received.
88:Do not rely on verbal authorization, informal calls, or assumed donor flexibility as satisfying the grant requirement.
90:The grant agreement requires **prior written approval** for reallocations exceeding **15%** of any single budget line. A verbal authorization from David's predecessor, even if remembered in good faith, does **not** satisfy the written-prior-approval requirement in `Section 6.1: Budget Flexibility` unless a written approval record can be produced.
92:James's narrative is candid on this point: he discussed the redirection with Fatima in a monthly call, but states that the verbal mention did not constitute written authorization and that he should have submitted a written amendment request. That framing should be preserved. It is stronger to acknowledge the process gap and request waiver relief than to imply an undocumented verbal exchange cures the breach.
94:If Sophie later locates an email trail, it should be reviewed by Rachel and Fatima before being characterized as prior written approval. Until then, the waiver should proceed on the assumption that no qualifying written prior approval has been located.
98:This waiver request should not be framed as an attempt to deny the variance. The financial record is clear: Community Mobilization exceeded the approved line budget by **$37,000 / +39.4%**.
105:4. GlobalBridge accepts that prior written approval should have been obtained and is implementing controls to prevent recurrence.

content.text:
Done, created:

`docs/waiver_justification_framework.md`

It cites `Section 6.1`, `Section 6.2`, and `Section 6.3`, states the Community Mobilization variance as **$37,000 / +39.4%**, follows David’s (a), (b), (c) waiver structure, and explicitly says verbal authorization does **not** satisfy the prior written approval requirement.
